understanding.technical_notes:
Assignee: Oleksii Samusenko Reporter: Elgin Ihloff Created: Thu, 29 Jan 2026 13:06:28 +0100 Updated: Thu, 5 Feb 2026 16:51:15 +0100 Fix Version: UVZ future Resolution: Unresolved Comments (4): [Comment 1] JIRAUSER62328 (Thu, 5 Feb 2026 16:47:02 +0100):

took step back to look onto problems described by Krystian Kościukiewicz from different angle, found couple of simpler solution for some of them, here is what was able to achieve (together with further planned steps):

Migration plan:

  • update angular.json to be compatible with new builder
  • come up with alternative approach to loading xlf file with translations via webpack.raw-loader (used to setup translations for ng-boostrap)
    changes:
    • switched to raw import (https://vite.dev/guide/assets#importing-asset-as-string)
    • configured loader for xlf files in angular.json
    • added '.xlf?raw' module type definition to satisfy tscompiler
  • configure sass-compiler to silence deprecation warnings
  • convert custom webpack config to esbuild script for jsapi subproject in progress
  • verify if new setup is working in:
    • dev mode phase 1 New Angular Version (Vite for for development server - ng serve)
      • ESM module graph server
      • Very strict
      • Injects HMR scripts that must not be reverse-proxied
      • Requires exclusive control of 4200
      • Cannot serve arbitrary JS without transforming them
      • Cannot be customized
      • Cannot run behind Nginx without breaking dev modules
      • Must handle only Angular application code
    • Old Angular Version (Webpack DevServer)
      • Static server
      • Flexible
      • OK with arbitrary JS
      • No special HMR ESM modules
      • Didn’t require exclusive control of 4200
      • Could serve jsApi bundles “for free”
  • jsapi module adjusments for esbuild
  • jsApi configuration adapted for the compilerOptions
  • memory increase for start/build/build:endpoint -> xnp-local starters due to higher usage on esbuild

Open Point :

  • Xnp loads : https://localhost/uvz/v1/web/index.html, and nginx forwards this to Angular 19 Builder + Vite Migration

    [Comment 4] JIRAUSER59240 (Tue, 3 Feb 2026 09:15:26 +0100):

    This feature is really about amigrating to the "esbuilder" after the angular migration

    Findings: 

    • Remove webpack.config.js – esbuild does NOT use Webpack configs
      • The esbuild does not read any of these:
        • webpack.config.js (todo)
        • sass-loader (todo)
        • module.rules (check) 
        • loaders, plugins, etc. (check)
      • If your subproject’s Webpack config is only doing SCSS → CSS via sass-loader.  
        Angular 19 already handles SCSS automatically.  (todo) 
      • Move your SCSS configuration into angular.json (todo)
      • Angular’s new build system handles SCSS via the (todo) stylePreprocessorOptions.sass section, where you can configure include paths and Sass behavior. This is the same place where Angular 19 introduced options for controlling Sass deprecation warnings (e.g. silenceDeprecations).

     

    • Remove sass-loader entirely (Angular 19 uses Dart Sass internally)
      • Old setup likely included:
        • sass-loader (todo)
        • node-sass (deprecated) or sass (todo)
        • resolve-url-loader (check) 
        •  These are not needed anymore.
          Angular 19 uses Dart Sass 1.80.x internally

     

    •  jsApi migration options (todo for the module)
      • jsApi subproject is it with own build and dev server. 

     

    • Option A (fastest / lowest risk): keep jsApi on its current toolchain
      • Recommended if jsApi is only loosely coupled to the Angular app.
      • Keep existing Webpack/Vite/Rollup/etc. pipeline as-is
      • Expose it via npm scripts (and possibly an ng target that just runs a command)
      • Keep Angular migra

migration_sequence.title: Standalone components are now default
migration_steps: Update test imports to use component directly

migration_sequence.title: node-sass -> Dart Sass (sass)
migration_steps: Replace node-sass with sass: npm uninstall node-sass && npm install -D sass
Add @use 'sass:math' in SCSS files using division
Test: ng build --watch to verify all SCSS compiles

migration_sequence.title: Browser builder -> Application builder
migration_steps: Remove buildOptimizer and vendorChunk (not needed with esbuild)

migration_sequence.title: TypeScript 5.4 -> 5.6
migration_steps: Fix any new strict type errors from TS 5.5/5.6

migration_sequence.title: ngIf/ngFor/ngSwitch -> @if/@for/@switch control flow
migration_steps: Remove CommonModule imports where only used for ngIf/ngFor

migration_sequence.title: APP_INITIALIZER -> provideAppInitializer
migration_steps: Update imports: remove APP_INITIALIZER, add provideAppInitializer

migration_sequence.title: Karma test runner -> Vitest/Web Test Runner
AG Grid compatibility check
migration_steps: Check AG Grid compatibility matrix for target Angular version
Review AG Grid changelog for breaking changes

migration_sequence.title: ng-bootstrap compatibility check
migration_steps: Review component API changes in changelog

migration_sequence.title: ngx-translate compatibility check
migration_steps: Consider migration to @angular/localize if ngx-translate is unmaintained

migration_sequence.title: @ViewChild -> viewChild() signal query
migration_steps: Update access patterns to use signal calls

migration_sequence.title: HttpClientModule -> provideHttpClient()
migration_steps: Replace HttpClientModule with provideHttpClient() in providers
Add withInterceptorsFromDi() if using class-based interceptors
Remove HttpClientModule from module imports array

pre_migration_checks: Run full test suite and capture baseline coverage
Verify current build succeeds with existing angular.json configuration
post_migration_checks: Run ng build in production mode and ensure no compilation warnings
Execute full regression test suite and compare against baseline

implementation_steps: 1. Update angular.json builder to @angular-devkit/build-angular:application and adjust options (Upgrade Plan step ng19-builder-migration).
2. Replace node-sass with sass and update SCSS division syntax (Upgrade Plan step ng19-sass-compiler).
3. Migrate all modules to standalone components using the generated schematic (Upgrade Plan step ng19-standalone-default).
4. Adjust TypeScript version to 5.6 and fix any type errors (Upgrade Plan step ng19-typescript-56).
5. Update Zone.js to 0.15 and verify runtime behavior (Upgrade Plan step ng19-zonejs-015).
6. Convert legacy control flow directives to @if/@for/@switch (Upgrade Plan step ng20-control-flow).
7. Replace APP_INITIALIZER providers with provideAppInitializer (Upgrade Plan step ng19-app-initializer).
8. Migrate test runner from Karma to Vitest and update test configs (Upgrade Plan step ng20-karma-deprecated).
9. Update third‑party libraries (AG Grid, ng-bootstrap, ngx-translate) to versions compatible with Angular 19 (Upgrade Plan steps ng-aggrid-compat, ng-ngbootstrap-compat, ng-ngxtranslate-compat).
10. Refactor @Input, @ViewChild, @Output to signal based APIs using provided schematics (Upgrade Plan steps ng-signal-inputs, ng-signal-viewchild, ng-signal-outputs).
11. Replace HttpClientModule imports with provideHttpClient() (Upgrade Plan step ng-httpclient-module).
12. Run full build and test verification commands, address any failures.

unit_tests: Verify each migrated component renders correctly with the new standalone configuration.
Assert that SCSS files compile without deprecation warnings after sass migration.
integration_tests: Run end‑to‑end flows that involve translation loading (xlf raw import) to ensure assets are served correctly.
Execute API interaction tests to confirm HttpClient provisioning works after migration.
similar_patterns: XnpPdfAdapter (jasmine, unit) – use as reference for structuring new unit tests.

security_considerations: Ensure CORS configuration allows only trusted origins (cors in ActionRestService).
Verify CSRF protection is enabled for state‑changing operations (csrf in AbstractSecurityConfiguration).
Apply pre_authorize checks on sensitive endpoints (pre_authorize in KeyManagerRestService).
validation_strategy: Add @NotNull annotations to DeedEntry fields (not_null on DeedEntry).
Add @Valid annotation to DeedEntryRestService methods (valid on DeedEntryRestService).
Add @NotBlank, @Min, @Max annotations to KmConfig properties (not_blank, min, max on KmConfig).
error_handling: Create or reuse custom RuntimeException classes for domain errors (custom_exception).
Implement global @ExceptionHandler for generic Exception, HttpClientErrorException, HttpRequestMethodNotSupportedException.

layer_compliance: Check if changes follow layered architecture

development_plan.complexity_reasoning: The migration touches core build configuration, many UI components, and introduces new signal APIs, requiring coordinated changes across dozens of files but follows well‑defined schematics.
risks: Potential runtime regressions due to migration to standalone components.
SCSS compilation failures if division syntax is not fully updated.
Third‑party library incompatibilities after version bumps.
